semantic_text:
feat(accounts): move staff model and related files to accounts app

feat(accounts): add JWT authentication

feat: implement JWT authentication with httpOnly cookies for user profile

This commit introduces JWT authentication using httpOnly cookies for enhanced security and improved user profile management.

- Adds UserProfileSerializer to serialize user profile data.
- Implements get_current_user view to retrieve the current user's profile.
- Implements logout_user view to clear JWT cookies upon logout.
- Configures JWT settings to use httpOnly cookies for access and refresh tokens.
- Updates authentication classes to use JWTAuthentication.
- Modifies JWTAuthentication class to extract token from httpOnly cookie.
- Adds configuration options to enable/disable JWT and dual authentication.

refactor: update models, views, and utils across multiple apps

- Modified job validation command and serializers
- Added new utility files for job and timesheet apps
- Updated purchasing models and services
- Renamed and reorganized delivery receipt and timesheet commands
- Removed unused workflow filters and tables
- Improved workflow models, utils, and Xero views

fix(migrations): change related name to workflow.purchaseorder

The related name was pointing to purchasing.purchaseorder,
which is incorrect since the model is now in the workflow app.
feat(migrations): add help_text to DateField in Stock model

The DateField in the Stock model now has a help_text attribute
to provide more context to the user.

refactor: remove job field from jobpart model and stock models

The job field was removed from the JobPart model because it was redundant and unnecessary. The stock models were refactored to remove unnecessary fields and models related to purchase orders and supplier quotes. A proxy model for Stock was created to maintain compatibility with the purchasing app.

fix(purchasing): rename 'purchase_orders_create' url name to 'edit_purchase_order'

The URL pattern for creating a new purchase order is being reused
for editing existing purchase orders. To reflect this, the name of
the URL pattern is changed to 'edit_purchase_order'.
fix(advanced_search.js): fix fetch url for clients api

fix(migrations): remove redundant database operations

This commit removes redundant database operations from a
migration file to ensure that the migration process is
efficient and avoids unnecessary actions.
fix(migrations): change purchasing app name to workflow in ForeignKey to fix circular dependency

refactor(apps/purchasing/views): Improve code organization and structure

This commit refactors the purchasing app's views to improve code organization and maintainability.  It introduces a more modular structure by separating views into individual files based on their functionality (purchase orders, delivery receipts, stock management).  This enhances readability and makes it easier to maintain and extend the codebase.  Each view file now follows a consistent naming convention and includes a clear docstring describing its purpose.  The changes also improve the separation of concerns by moving some logic into dedicated service classes.

feat: integrate django-apscheduler for scheduled tasks

This commit introduces django-apscheduler to schedule scraper tasks.
It adds the django-apscheduler dependency to pyproject.toml.
The scheduler jobs are moved to a separate file,
quoting/scheduler_jobs.py, to ensure they can be properly serialized.
The QuotingConfig now imports the scheduler-related modules and
the standalone job functions within the ready method.
The scheduler is configured to run the scraper job every Sunday at 3 PM NZT
and to clean up old job executions daily.
fix(base.html): fix url for kpi_calendar to include the accounting namespace

refactor: update import paths for enum utils in purchasing app
feat(purchase_order_list.html): improve UI of purchase order list view
feat: remove unused purchase order form template

feat(apps/accounts): Implement custom token obtain and refresh views

This commit introduces custom token obtain and refresh views for JWT authentication.  The custom views handle password reset requirements and set JWT tokens as HttpOnly cookies for enhanced security, improving the overall security of the authentication process.  The `CustomTokenObtainPairView` now sets JWT tokens as HttpOnly cookies if enabled in settings, enhancing security.  The `CustomTokenRefreshView` retrieves the refresh token from a cookie if not provided in the request data, improving user experience and security.

refactor(apps/accounts/urls.py): Remove unused TokenRefreshView import

This commit removes the unused `TokenRefreshView` import from `apps/accounts/urls.py`, improving code cleanliness and maintainability.

feat(apps/job): Add Kanban service layer

This commit introduces a new service layer for Kanban functionality, encapsulating all business logic related to job management in the Kanban board.  This improves code organization and maintainability.

refactor(apps/job/urls.py): Migrate Kanban endpoints to API

This commit migrates all Kanban endpoints to the API, improving the structure and consistency of the application.

feat(apps/job/views): Create Kanban API views

This commit creates new API views for Kanban board functionality, handling requests for fetching jobs, updating job statuses, reordering jobs, and performing advanced searches.  This improves the API's functionality and organization.

refactor(apps/job/views): Remove old Kanban views

This commit removes the old Kanban views, which are no longer needed after migrating to the API.  This improves code cleanliness and maintainability.

fix(apps/workflow): Correct client model path in fixture

This commit corrects the path to the `Client` model in the `initial_data.json` fixture, fixing a potential issue with data loading.

refactor(jobs_manager/authentication.py): Improve JWT authentication

This commit improves JWT authentication to support both Authorization header and HttpOnly cookies, providing flexibility and enhanced security.

refactor(jobs_manager/settings): Configure JWT authentication

This commit configures JWT authentication, enabling it for API requests and allowing for dual authentication (session and JWT) if desired.  It also configures JWT cookie settings for local development, allowing non-HTTPS cookies for easier development.  The `ENABLE_JWT_AUTH` setting controls whether JWT authentication is enabled.  The `ENABLE_DUAL_AUTHENTICATION` setting controls whether both session and JWT authentication are enabled.  The JWT cookie settings are overridden for local development to allow non-HTTPS cookies.  Session and CSRF cookie settings are also adjusted for local development to allow non-HTTPS cookies.

refactor(workflow): remove file_service and job_service

The `file_service.py` and `job_service.py` files were removed.  This was done to improve the overall structure and maintainability of the codebase.  The functionality previously provided by these services will be refactored and integrated elsewhere.

feat(workflow): add migration to alter Stock model

This commit introduces a migration to modify the
`source_purchase_order_line` field in the `Stock`
model.  The change updates the field to use the
fully qualified path to the `PurchaseOrderLine`
model, improving clarity and maintainability.  The
help text is also improved for better understanding.

feat(base.html): move KPI Reports url to accounting namespace

The reports_index url was removed and the KPI Reports url was moved to the accounting namespace.
This change was made to improve the organization of the urls and to make it easier to find the KPI Reports url.
fix(migrations): update related_name to point to workflow app

refactor: move InvoiceStatus and QuoteStatus enums to accounting app

The InvoiceStatus and QuoteStatus enums were located in the job app,
but they are actually used in the accounting app. This commit moves
them to the accounting app to improve the project structure and
maintainability.
